title: Kazak

{{Dialogue|Kazak|Chief Chirpa, you go ''too'' far! You have no ''right'' to do this!|Chief Chirpa|How ''dare'' you speak to me like that, ''Kazak?''|Kazak|As ''First Elder'' of the Ewok Council, I must remind you that all ''Ewok'' leaders have been '''men!''' After all, the leader of the Ewoks should at least be ''sturdy enough'' to carry a '''spear''' into battle!|Logray|Perhaps the noble council members are simply ''afraid'' to be led by a woman!|Kazak|You have no voice here, Logray! Stay with your ''spells'' and ''potions!''|attr=Kazak disputes Chief Chirpa's decision to pass the tribe's leadership to his daughter Princess Kneesaa|src=<ref name="e2">[[Ewoks (1985) 2|''Ewoks'' (1985) 2]]</ref>}}
'''Kazak''' was [[First Elder]] of the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[Council of Elders (Ewok)/Legends|Council of Elders]] and an advisor to [[Chief/Legends|Chief]] [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]]. He was light-gray/blue fur skinned and wore a brown hood. He was also one of the many Ewoks that aided the Rebel Alliance in the Battle of Endor.

His name originated in Ewok Mythology, in which Kazak was a nature spirit who rewards Ewoks with magic weapons if they defeat him at his complicated games.

==Biography==
===Space pirates invade===
Kazak was an [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] member of the [[Bright Tree Village tribe]] on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. He was the [[Head Elder]] for [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village's]] [[Council of Elders (Ewok)/Legends|Council of Elders]],<ref name="e2" /> acting as an advisor to [[Chief/Legends|Chief]] [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]].<ref name="CCG">{{CCG|set=Endor Limited|cardname=Kazak}}</ref> In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="e date">{{InsiderCite|23|Star Wars Publications Timeline|''Star Wars'' Publications Timeline}} dates the events of the [[Star Wars: Ewoks (1985)|''Ewoks'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic series]], which include the events of [[Ewoks (1985) 2|''Ewoks'' 2]], to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], which corresponds to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> at the [[Birthday/Legends|birthday]] party of [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]]—daughter of Chirpa—Kazak was present when Chirpa declared that Kintaka would be next in line for leader of the Ewoks. Kazak was apalled by the chief's statement, reminding him of the tradition of male Ewok leaders. He tried to prove his point that a womna leader would be unfit to serve by tossing a [[Spear/Legends|spear]] at Kintaka, who was barely able to catch it. The [[Shaman/Legends|medicine man]] [[Logray/Legends|Logray]] countered by implying that the council was frightened of the prospect of a female leader, but Kazak shot him down, remdinding him to stick to his [[Magic/Legends|magic]] and nothing else. Chirpa then insisted that Kintaka's [[birthright]] was to lead; Kazak allowed for the chief to protest the council's feelings, but he maintained that it would be the elders who chose the next leader—not Chirpa.<ref name="e2" />

Later that [[Standard day/Legends|day]], the village was invaded by a group of [[Zorbian space pirates]]<ref name="e2" /> from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Zorbia II]].<ref name="Gamer">{{GamerCite|9|Endor and the Moddell Sector}}</ref> The council, which had been preparing to leave the birthday party, learned of this when the Ewok [[Scout/Legends|scout]] [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Warrick]] burst in with the news, informing everyone of the [[Pirate/Legends|pirates']] ill intent. Chirpa called everyone to arms, but he made his daughter stay behind. Kintaka accused him of siding with Kazak and the elders, but her [[Parent/Legends|father]] simply deferred the conversation to later. However, the Ewok warriors ran into a trap, becoming captured by the Zorbians, who tied them all up in a net and brought them back to the village, where they intended to [[Thief/Legends|steal]] the precious [[gemwood tree]]. Kintaka, among the few Ewoks left, had devised a plan for defeating the pirates; she and her Ewok friends used items from around the village to fight the Zorbians and drive them away.<ref name="e2" />

After the battle, when Chirpa told his daughter how proud he was of her, Kazak concurred, offering an apology for his earlier comments. On behalf of the council, he proclaimed that Kintaka was already on her way to becoming a strong leader, and he presented her with a [[ruling staff]]. Kintaka accepted the honor and vowed to continue to prove herself worthy.<ref name="e2" /> Sometime later, Kazak presented Kintaka, Warrick, and their friend [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]] with special stone [[Knife/Legends|blades]] to cut the Ewoks' [[sacred wood]], which they considered an honor. The [[Youngling/Legends|young]] Ewoks then departed via [[Hang-glider/Legends|hang gliders]] to gather the [[Wood/Legends|wood]].<ref name="e3">[[Ewoks (1985) 3|''Ewoks'' (1985) 3]]</ref>

===A test of courage===
Later that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], Kazak was present to officiate a test of courage for Wicket Warrick. The young Ewok was to swing on a [[Vine/Legends|vine]] as part of the test, but the [[Rope/Legends|rope]] was cut by the [[Dulok/Legends|Dulok]] [[Prince/Legends|prince]] [[Boogutt]], causing him to fall and destroy the Ewoks' special [[sun crystal]]. This greatly angered Kazak; the elder felt that Warrick had been carelss and was thus unworthy of being a warrior, everyone all involved was unaware of the Duloks' intervention. Afterward, Warrick and Teebo overheard Kazak and Chirpa discussing the events; Chirpa thought that Kazak had been overly harsh, but the elder defended his position, reminding him of the hundred years that the [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] had been an important symbol for the Ewoks. Despite Chirpa pointing out that it was just a symbol and nothing more, Kazak scoffed and opined that the Ewok warriors of old who had retrieved the crystal from the [[Black Cavern]] were truly courageous.<ref name="e13">[[Ewoks (1985) 13|''Ewoks'' (1985) 13]]</ref>

Kazak's rant inspired Warrick to prove himself by obtaining a new sun crystal from the Black Cavern. He ran off to do so, with Boogutt following, also trying to get the crystal—in his case, for his [[Parent/Legends|father]], [[King/Legends|King]] [[Gorneesh/Legends|Gorneesh]]. Teebo learned of this and informed the elders, who gathered a party to chase after Warrick and prevent him from risking his life. After narrowly escaping being trapped inside the [[Cave/Legends|cave]] forever, and saving Boogutt's life in the process, the Ewok party arrived at the cavern's entrance, though the sun crystal was destroyed. Warrick was devastated at losing two sun crystals in a single day, Kazak surprised him by implying that perhaps the symbol no longer mattered; it was courage that defined an Ewok, and Warrick's would be long-remembered. He then presented the young scout with a shard of the broken crystal for his [[Belt of Honor]], leaving him speechless. The Ewoks then all returned to Bright Tree Village.<ref name="e13" />

===Imperials on Endor===
[[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]<ref name="GG5">''[[Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi]]''</ref> in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of {{Film|VI}} to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].</ref> the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] sent a scouting party to Endor and eventually brought numerous [[Trooper/Legends|troops]] and vehicles.<ref name="GG5" /> Kazak was part of a [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] party that investigated a trap that had been set. However, instead of a creature being caught in the net, it was a group of Rebels from the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. The group included the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], who the Ewoks bowed down to, thinking him to be a [[Deity/Legends|deity]]. The Ewoks brought their captives and new deity back to Bright Tree Village, where they nearly sacrificed the live Rebels before C-3PO told them not to.<ref name="rotj" />

The next day, the Ewoks accompanied the Rebels in trying to disable the [[Endor shield generator bunker]] so that the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] could destroy the Empire's [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]].<ref name="rotj" /> Kazak assisted by studying the local Imperial patrols, devising strategies to attack the opposing forces.<ref name="CCG" />

==Behind the scenes==
Kazak was created for the [[Ewoks (1985) 2|second issue]] of the [[Star Wars: Ewoks (1985)|''Ewoks'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic series]], which was written by [[David Manak]], illustrated by [[Warren Kremer]], and published by [[Marvel Comics]]<ref name="e2" /> on [[April 2]], [[1985]].<ref name="Age">{{BookCite|title=[[Marvel Age]]|number=Vol. 1 No. 27|publisher=[[Marvel Comics]] Group|date=[[June]] [[1985]]}}</ref> The [[1999]] expansion to [[Decipher]]'s [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]] [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] Kazak<ref name="CCG" /> as having been one of the Ewoks in the [[1983]] [[original trilogy]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']].<ref name="rotj" />
